title: Build a Crystal Radio
body: A working radio with no batteries — runs on the energy of the radio waves themselves. The first DIY electronics project a generation of American kids built.
bands: A galena crystal (or a 1N34A germanium diode as substitute)
Several yards of insulated copper wire
A cardboard tube (toilet paper or oatmeal canister)
Earphones (high-impedance vintage; or use a crystal earpiece)
Long wire antenna (15+ feet) and a ground connection
Small variable capacitor or a slider made from foil
author: engine
witness_status_reason: no registry entry matched 'Popular Science, March 1922 (and many others)'; only the source itself attests
